title: Fix MongoDB Query to Handle Missing Fields

statement.markdown: Write a MongoDB query function that takes a query object and a document, and returns the fields from the document that match the query. If the query specifies a field that doesn't exist, the function should ignore it and continue processing the rest of the query. If the query specifies a field with an empty value, the function should also ignore it. The function should return all fields from the document if the query is empty.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be two parameters: a query object and a document.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be an object with the fields from the document that match the query.
statement.notes: The function should not throw any errors if the query specifies a field that doesn't exist or has an empty value.

constraints.description: Number of fields in the query
Number of fields in the document

examples.explanation: The query matches all fields in the document
The query matches field "a" but not field "b" because its value is null
The query is empty, so the function returns all fields from the document

hints.text: Check if the query is empty before iterating over it

functionSignature.python: def queryFields(self, query: dict, document: dict) -> dict:
